“Sentio me esse, ergo sum” (I perceive existence, therefore I am)
Vs
“Sentire esse, ergo esse” (perception of existence, therefore existence)

“I”ing is another extra thing happening in awareness, like thinking.
So there is a sense in which “am” can stand alone. Just like your existence can be recognized independent of thought, existence seems to be recognizable independent of self.

Cogito ergo sum has always seemed like a weird argument to me. If you’re allowed to notice “I think” and then back into a conclusion, why can’t you just notice “I am” directly? 
More like, “I think therefore I am conceptually aware that I am” amirite.

Of course if close attention is paid to experience, the whole “I” part of this “I am” thing turns out to be fuzzier than expected. There is an active ongoing process of constructing the “I” that can be observed.

there is already some implicit exchange rate that you are using!

the subjective probability people are just saying (or proposing) exchange rates they use

say there are two possible worlds, A and not-A

and say you have an opportunity to make a tradeoff that benefits you if A at some (possibly just opportunity) cost to you if not-A

what is the exchange rate you use to decide?

THAT'S your subjective probability

when you make up a subjective probability, the math comes AFTER not before

it's not that you used some magic rigorous process to give you a super precise number

it's that your future *decision* tradeoffs about possible worlds can be described using a specific number

The particles of our bodies don't fail of course, they were following the Way at all times. So in that sense we always do as well. But in the higher dimensional space that the Living Way exists, we fail.
Anyway this is why @ESYudkowsky  is basically a Dath Ilani Daoist and you shouldn't believe him when he tries to claim he's not.

We still follow the Way, there is only one Way to follow, but we follow it only approximately. On the balance it's a good investment, we find free energy minimas far deeper than a greedy approach could find. But in trade we also screw up and fail.

Living beings are not so perfect, because we strive to do better than water and light and in doing so risk failure. Living things may deliberately choose to go against their expectations today, for the chance at a better tomorrow. We speculate.
Evolution follows the Way, as it explores the space of all living things seeking, finding the path of least action through species-space.

An LLM knows a little of the Way, though less than a living being, for in striving to minimize prediction error on the next token it is still striving to reduce the expected free energy of its predictions.

To a being in a sufficiently high-dimensional space, the Way is a simple thing, unadorned and straightforward. Following it is no more difficult than water running down hill. But projected to the level of our observations, it shatters into a million facets.

Nonetheless the underlying pattern is there. I will commit the sin that everyone who writes of the Way must commit: I will attempt to name and grasp it. I name it as the way of least action, which is the way of minimizing free energy, which is the way of maximizing energy.

Because there's only one reality, there is but one Way that dances correctly with the truth. Peer closely enough at your goal and it appears, vivid and inarguable. Grasp to define it and it shifts, like a rainbow receding from you as you approach.

For an electron, the Way is the Langrangian.
For the universe, the Way is the 2nd law of thermodynamics.
For a living being, the Way is constantly striving to learn what to expect from the universe and also to bend the universe to what you expect.

I am against degrowth, and have been ever since reading Pournelle's "A Step Farther Out" as a kid.  This doesn't mean I'm okay with invalid gotchas against degrowth; the valid arguments are more than enough.

This is why it can be deceptively damaging to avoid believing heresy. Heresy spreads through entanglement in your web of beliefs, and the deeper your understanding, the more entangled your web of beliefs.

Some fun heresy logic:

If Q is heretical and P, then (P -> Q) is heretical. 

If Q is heretical and (P -> Q), then P is heretical. 

If Q is heretical and (P or Q), then ~P is heretical.
